title: Risk-Calibrated Human-Robot Interaction via Set-Valued Intent Prediction
abstract: This paper studies the tradeoff between risk and autonomy in human-robot interaction, particularly in cluttered, close-quarters environments. The authors propose a risk-calibrated approach that combines calibrated predict-then-plan methods with deep-learned human intent prediction models to enable safe robot behavior.

methodology.prediction: Deep-learned human intent prediction models with confidence-aware outputs
methodology.planning: Sequence of actions planned for each predicted intent
methodology.calibration: Use of calibration dataset to select hyperparameters
methodology.human_robot_interaction: Robot asks for help when uncertain

problem.main_challenge: Human intent prediction is challenging due to individual preferences and environmental factors
problem.specific_issue: Situational ambiguity where items may fit multiple categories
problem.tradeoff: Balance between robot autonomy and risk

key_contributions: Calibration of prediction models for risk management
applications: Item sorting tasks (e.g., sorting vegetables, toys, miscellaneous items)
Safe human-robot interaction in cluttered environments

technical_details.hyperparameters: Temperature and other flexible parameters to control confidence levels
technical_details.prediction_output: Limited set of human intents based on confidence scores
technical_details.planning_objective: Satisfy environment objectives (e.g., placing items in correct bins)
technical_details.risk_objectives: Statistical risk calibration and limiting multiple notions of risk
